zh.lifeSummary: Hannah Warner於1802年8月10日出生在美國紐約州阿爾班尼波納。她於1823年6月12日與Jothan Wheelock在美國紐約州結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她在美國紐約州艾利蘭卡斯特蘭卡斯特住了大約5年。她去世於1858年5月29日享年55歲，埋葬在美國紐約州艾利布費阿洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Hannah Warner于1802年8月10日出生在美国纽约州阿爾班尼波納。她于1823年6月12日与Jothan Wheelock在美国纽约州结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她在美国纽约州艾利蘭卡斯特蘭卡斯特住了大约5年。她去世于1858年5月29日享年55岁，埋葬在美国纽约州艾利布費阿洛。

nameMeanings.name: Warner
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and North German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements war(in) ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’ + heri, hari ‘army’. The name was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Warnier (Old French Garnier). Compare Garner and Werner .
English (of Norman origin): shortened form of Warrener (see Warren 2).

Irish (Cork): when this is not the Anglo-Norman name (see above), an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin (see Murnane ), found in medieval records as Iwarrynane, from a genitive or plural form of the name, in which m is lenited.

variants.name: Garner
meanings: English (of Norman origin): perhaps occasionally from the Old French personal name Garnier (see Garnier ), but it is exeptionally rare as a personal name in medieval England and no certain evidence has been found for its use as a surname. Compare Warner .

English: from Middle English gern(i)er, garner, gurner, Anglo-Norman French gerner ‘granary’ (Old French grenier, from Late Latin granarium, a derivative of granum ‘grain’). It was probably a metonymic occupational name for someone in charge of the stores kept in a granary.
English and Scottish: commonly shortened form of   Gardner  .

variants.name: Werner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Erwin, Heinz, Gerhard, Ernst, Helmut, Horst, Klaus, Arno, Dieter.

German, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Hungarian, Norwegian, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’ + heri, hari ‘army’. The surname of German origin is also found in Britain (compare Warner ) and France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Verner .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Hungarian, Swedish, Danish, Croatian, and Slovenian   Verner  , a cognate or variant of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Hannah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the mother of the prophet Samuel (1 Samuel 1:2), Hebrew Hanna. It is derived from a Hebrew word meaning ‘He (i.e. God) has favoured me (i.e. with a child)’. See also Anne . This form of the name was taken up as a given name by the Puritans in the 16th and 17th centuries and remained popular until the late 19th century. Thereafter it fell somewhat from favour but has enjoyed a massive revival since the 1990s.

variants.name: Hanna
meanings: Either the biblical name ( see Hannah ) or a short form of Johanna .

variants.name: Hana
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.description: France sells Louisiana territories to U.S.A.

historicEvents.title: The U.S doubles in size
historicEvents.description: The United States purchased all the Louisiana territory (828,000 sq. mi) from France, only paying 15 million dollars (A quarter trillion today) for the land. In the purchase, the US obtained the land that makes up 15 US states and 2 Canadian Provinces. The United States originally wanted to purchase of New Orleans and the lands located on the coast around it, but quickly accepted the bargain that Napoleon Bonaparte offered.

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.
Historical Boundaries: 1821: Erie, New York, United States

historicEvents.title: The Black Hawk War
historicEvents.description: Convinced that a group of Native American tribes were hostile, The United States formed a frontier militia to stop them in their tracks. Even though Black Hawk was hoping to avoid bloodshed while trying to resettle on tribal land, U.S. officials opened fire on the Native Americans. Black Hawk then responded to this confrontation by successfully attacking the militia at the Battle of Stillman's Run and then left northward. After a few months the militia caught up with Black Hawk and his men and defeated them at the Battle of Wisconsin Heights. While being weakened by hunger, injuries and desertion, Black Hawk and the rest of the many native survivors retreated towards the Mississippi. Unfortunately, Black Hawk and other leaders were later captured when they surrendered to the US forces and were then imprisoned for a year.
U.S. acquires vast tracts of Mexican territory in wake of Mexican War including California and New Mexico.

en.lifeSummary: Hannah Warner was born on 10 August 1802, in Berne, Albany, New York, United States. She married Jothan Wheelock on 12 June 1823, in New York, United States. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 5 daughters. She lived in Lancaster, Lancaster, Erie, New York, United States for about 5 years. She died on 29 May 1858, at the age of 55, and was buried in Buffalo, Erie, New York, United States.

ja.lifeSummary: Hannah Warnerは1802年8月10日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州アルバニーバーンで生まれた。1823年6月12日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州でJothan Wheelockと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女はアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州エリーランカスターランカスターに約5年間住んでいた。彼女は1858年5月29日に55歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州エリーブファロにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hannah Warner(은)는 1802년 8월 10일 미국 뉴욕 주 알바니 버니에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1823년 6월 12일 미국 뉴욕 주에서 Jothan Wheelock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 미국 뉴욕 주 에리 랭커스터 랭커스터에서 약 5년 동안 살았다. 그녀는 1858년 5월 29일 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 뉴욕 주 에리 버팔로에 묻혔다.

pt.10 August 1802–29 May 1858: 10 de agosto de 1802–29 de maio de 1858

pt.lifeSummary: Hannah Warner nasceu em 10 de agosto de 1802, em Berne, Albany, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América. Ela casou-se com Jothan Wheelock em 12 de junho de 1823, em Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 5 filhas. Ela viveu em Lancaster, Lancaster, Erie, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América por quase 5 anos. Ela faleceu em 29 de maio de 1858, com 55 anos, e foi sepultada em Buffalo, Erie, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.E.D. 2, Lancaster, Erie, New York, Unite: ланкастир, ланкастир, эри, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Hannah Warner родилась 10 августа 1802 г. в местности бирн, албани, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Jothan Wheelock 12 июня 1823 г. в местности Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 5 дочерей. Она жила в местности ланкастир, ланкастир, эри, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты около 5 лет. Она умерла 29 мая 1858 г. в возрасте 55 лет и была похоронена в местности буффалоу, эри, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Hannah Warner nació el 10 de agosto de 1802, en Berne, Albany, Nueva York, Estados Unidos. Se casó con Jothan Wheelock el 12 de junio de 1823, en Nueva York, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 5 hijas. Vivió en Lancaster, Lancaster, Erie, Nueva York, Estados Unidos durante unos 5 años. Murió el 29 de mayo de 1858, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultada en Buffalo, Erie, Nueva York, Estados Unidos.
